summary: EXP-231 resolves ISS-1432 and ISS-1433 with two targeted detail_evidence substring patches on WIN-069. (1) Adds 90-Mile Straight (146.6 km, Balladonia to Caiguna) <0.4% anchor sentence so tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict's '0.4% deviation' figure now has a source in detail_evidence (closes ISS-1432 TLDR-detail anchor gap). (2) Extends the 'terrain-based routing models' sentence with an inline citation to Huber & Rust 2016 (R Journal) and Giacomin & Levinson 2015 (Transportation Planning and Technology), naming detour indices of 1.05–1.20 and sub-1% mean-ratio errors with one free parameter vs V13's ≥4 (closes ISS-1433 unsourced counter-model). Both 'before' anchors verified as exactly one occurrence in wins.json. No other WIN-069 fields touched. c3 Holes #1 (derives_from_dome tag↔text contradiction), #2 (10,000×/250,000× comparator endpoints), #5 (stale finding + same-hemisphere headline), and #6 (thousands-of-pairs arithmetic) remain open — each recommended as its own follow-up expansion due to conflicting anchors.
